description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 5 UI/manual cluster: reusable case documents vs live walkthrough vs evidence policy vs a11y-specific checks.

cases.prompt: Author step-by-step manual QA scripts with test data and expected results that any tester can execute for the billing pages.
cases.reason: Reusable stranger-executable scripts are manual case authoring — not a live session, and more granular than a test plan.

cases.prompt: Walk through the billing pages on the current build and report defects with screenshots today.
cases.reason: Driving the app now and reporting is a clickthrough session, not document authoring.

cases.prompt: Write the keyboard-only and screen-reader verification checklist for our forms.
cases.reason: A11y-specific manual checks (keyboard/focus/SR) belong to the accessibility harness, which owns both the automated and manual halves of that domain.

cases.prompt: Decide which checkpoints need screenshots for release evidence and the masking rules for them.
cases.reason: Evidence policy (checkpoints, naming, masking, storage) is the planner; cases only reference its rules.
